summary: This cluster focuses on the antioxidant and anti-lipid peroxidation properties of marine algae, specifically *Ecklonia cava* and *Pyropia yezoensis*, using both in vitro assays and in vivo zebrafish models. Key findings demonstrate that compounds such as phlorofucofuroeckol-A (PFF-A) from *E. cava* and crude extracts from specific Korean local strains of *P. yezoensis* effectively scavenge free radicals, inhibit reactive oxygen species (ROS), and reduce apoptosis and lipid peroxidation in a dose-dependent manner. The research highlights the significance of geographic origin, as the chemical composition and protective efficacy of *P. yezoensis* extracts varied among strains from different Korean coastal regions. Overall, these studies position brown and red algae as promising sources of natural antioxidants for mitigating oxidative stress-induced cellular damage.
